feedback.text: I hope you also want to tackle the big issue with publishers. This case concerns the fact that publishers now indicate that they do not sell games, but only grant a temporary licence. This means that publishers can withdraw their games at any time. You are no longer the owner of the product you bought. I see it just like a car you buy, but which can be taken away from you by the manufacturer at any time before the door, with a notice that it will be taken back. That it is not your car, but that you only paid for a licence to use the car and that it is now being withdrawn, without you being able to do anything to do so. This is not something we need to allow. I think we have to resist this and stop it so that products you buy are really your property.
